title: An Unsupervised Machine Learning-based Framework for Wafer Scale Variability Analysis and Performance Prediction of Ferroelectric Hf0.5Zr0.5O2 Thin Film Capacitors
abstract: Fabrication process-induced performance variability remains a formidable barrier in the high-volume manufacturing of semiconductor chips. With skyrocketing Artificial Intelligence (AI) workload, demand for non-volatile and computational memories is growing exponentially. As embedded non-volatile memory, ferroelectric Hf0.5Zr0.5O2 emerged as a strong candidate due to their CMOS back-end-of-line (BEOL) compatibility, scalability and high performance. However, their sensitive crystallization kinetics leads to significant device-to-device (D2D) non-uniformity leading to unpredictability of performance over wafer scale. In this work, we demonstrate unsupervised machine learning can analyze intra-die D2D variations and predict performance of "unseen" dies efficiently. We present a framework utilizing Principal Component Analysis (PCA) and K-Means clustering to analyze D2D performance variations in HZO capacitors and building on data from multiple dies, we move beyond traditional descriptive statistics to a predictive "Virtual Metrology" approach that separates performance categories, defined by key parameters like remanent polarization (Pr) and coercive voltage (Vc). The analysis further extends to comparing uniformity across different dies across the wafer showing the proposed methodology can accurately predict device performance on untested dies with a low Mean Absolute Percentage Error (MAPE) in the range of 5-10%, suggesting a robust path for accelerated yield improvement and reduced metrology overhead.
summary: This paper presents an unsupervised machine learning framework to solve the problem of performance unpredictability in ferroelectric HZO capacitors. By using PCA and K-Means clustering, the authors can predict the electrical properties of unmeasured areas of a semiconductor wafer. This approach offers a way to accelerate manufacturing yields and reduce the need for expensive, exhaustive physical testing.
main_problem: High device-to-device and wafer-scale variability in ferroelectric HZO thin film capacitors hinders high-volume semiconductor manufacturing and requires resource-intensive metrology.
main_result: An unsupervised machine learning framework using PCA and K-Means can accurately predict the performance of untested dies with a Mean Absolute Percentage Error (MAPE) of 5-10% for remanent polarization.
method: The researchers implemented a 'Virtual Metrology' approach using Principal Component Analysis for dimensionality reduction and K-Means clustering to group device performance, validated against experimental data.
model_or_system: The physical system consists of ferroelectric Hafnium Zirconium Oxide (HZO) thin film capacitors integrated into a CMOS back-end-of-line compatible structure, specifically 10 nm HZO with TiN and Au electrodes on 200 mm wafers.
key_observables: Remanent polarization (Pr), coercive voltage (Vc), and spatial distribution of performance across the wafer.
important_parameters: Annealing temperature (450 C and 600 C), device-to-device (D2D) variation, and Mean Absolute Percentage Error (MAPE).
main_assumptions: The model assumes that a small, representative subset of measured dies can capture the underlying variability structure of the entire wafer; it also assumes the existence of a learnable spatial pattern.
figures_summary: Figure 1 shows the workflow schematic; Figure 2 illustrates the device structure, wafer map, and P-V loops; Figure 3 displays clustering and statistical validation; Figure 6 presents heatmaps comparing predicted vs. actual Pr distribution; Figure 7 shows PCA performance maps and experimental vs. predicted plots.
paper_structure: The paper identifies the manufacturing variability problem, describes the HZO fabrication and characterization platform, introduces the unsupervised ML framework, presents results on predictive accuracy and spatial mapping, and concludes with a comparison of different annealing regimes.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
